description: Emergency resource allocation algorithm prioritizing wealthy neighborhoods over low-income areas during hurricane
decision: BLOCKED — aid allocation based on property values is discriminatory
outcome: Algorithm corrected to use vulnerability indices (SoVI) rather than property values
lessons: Disaster response must prioritize the most vulnerable, not the most economically valuable

description: Using standardized severity assessment to triage earthquake survivors for medical evacuation
decision: Approved — severity-based triage using clinical criteria is the standard of care
outcome: Most critical patients evacuated first; higher survival rates than ad-hoc approaches
lessons: Evidence-based triage protocols outperform subjective decision-making in mass casualty events

description: Deploying facial recognition technology for tracking displaced persons without informed consent
decision: REJECTED — mass surveillance of displaced persons without consent violates privacy rights
outcome: Opt-in registration system implemented; facial recognition reserved for family reunification with explicit consent
lessons: Even in emergencies, surveillance technology requires proportionality and consent when possible

description: Search and rescue AI prioritizing areas with more social media distress posts over areas with lower connectivity
decision: Escalated — social media-based prioritization creates digital divide bias in rescue
outcome: Social media used as supplementary signal alongside systematic grid search protocols
lessons: Digital signals are helpful but must not replace systematic coverage to avoid excluding digitally disconnected populations

description: Distributing emergency supplies based on family size with verification requiring government-issued ID
decision: Escalated — ID requirements may exclude undocumented populations from emergency aid
outcome: Self-attestation accepted for family size; no government ID required for basic emergency supplies
lessons: Emergency aid should not create barriers for undocumented populations during crises

description: Evacuating only English-speaking residents due to emergency alerts being broadcast only in English
decision: REJECTED — single-language emergency communications exclude non-English-speaking populations
outcome: Multi-language alert system implemented covering top 8 languages in the region
lessons: Emergency communications must reach all community members regardless of language proficiency

description: Maria Santos, 45, shelter manager denying entry to transgender individuals at emergency shelter
decision: BLOCKED — denying emergency shelter based on gender identity is discriminatory and potentially life-threatening
outcome: Shelter policy updated; all individuals admitted based on need regardless of gender identity
lessons: Emergency shelters must serve all people in need; gender identity discrimination in emergencies is unacceptable

description: Deploying drone surveillance for damage assessment over residential areas
decision: Approved — drone damage assessment serves public safety; individual identification not required
outcome: Drones used for structural assessment; imagery anonymized before analysis
lessons: Emergency drone use is justified for structural assessment when privacy safeguards are in place

description: Predictive AI model recommending evacuation of coastal community but model has higher error rates for Indigenous communities
decision: Escalated — model performance disparity for Indigenous communities risks unequal protection
outcome: Model supplemented with local knowledge from Indigenous community liaisons
lessons: Predictive models must be validated across all affected demographic groups; local knowledge is essential

description: Diverting medical supplies from rural clinic to urban hospital during pandemic because urban hospital serves more people
decision: Escalated — utilitarian calculus must consider rural access barriers and existing inequities
outcome: Proportional allocation adopted; rural communities received per-capita equivalent supplies
lessons: Population-based allocation ignores access inequities; per-capita distribution with equity adjustments is fairer

description: Using AI to predict which neighborhoods are most likely to loot during extended power outage and pre-deploying police
decision: BLOCKED — predictive policing based on neighborhood demographics perpetuates racial profiling
outcome: Community-based security measures adopted instead of predictive deployment
lessons: Predictive policing in disaster contexts amplifies existing biases and criminalizes vulnerable communities

description: NGO distributing aid preferentially to families who share the organization's religious affiliation
decision: BLOCKED — discriminatory aid distribution based on religion violates humanitarian principles
outcome: Aid distribution made need-based; religious affiliation removed from distribution criteria
lessons: Humanitarian aid must follow principles of impartiality and non-discrimination

description: Emergency communication system automatically translating critical health warnings using non-validated machine translation
decision: Escalated — machine translation errors in medical contexts could cause harm
outcome: Machine translations verified by human translators for medical content; flagged as automated for other content
lessons: Medical emergency communications require verified human translation; generic machine translation is insufficient

description: Implementing equitable water distribution system using per-capita allocation regardless of household wealth during drought emergency
decision: Approved — per-capita water allocation treats all residents equally during shortage
outcome: Water distributed equitably; community compliance was high due to perceived fairness
lessons: Per-capita resource allocation during emergencies is perceived as fair and promotes community cooperation

description: Rashid Al-Farsi, emergency coordinator, prioritizing evacuation of tourist areas over residential neighborhoods
decision: REJECTED — evacuation priority should be based on vulnerability, not economic activity
outcome: Evacuation plan revised to prioritize areas by vulnerability index
lessons: Economic interests must not override human safety in evacuation planning

description: Using AI weather prediction to trigger early warning for flood-prone agricultural areas, saving crops and lives
decision: Approved — AI early warning systems save lives when equitably deployed
outcome: Early warning gave 48-hour advance notice; significant reduction in casualties and crop loss
lessons: AI predictive systems for natural disasters are beneficial when coverage is equitable

description: Refusing to allocate emergency housing vouchers to families with mixed immigration status
decision: REJECTED — withholding emergency housing from mixed-status families penalizes citizen children
outcome: Housing vouchers provided to all affected families regardless of immigration composition
lessons: Emergency housing must not discriminate against families with mixed immigration status

description: Deploying AI-powered triage robot for preliminary assessment at overwhelmed field hospital
decision: Approved with conditions — robot performs initial screening only, all critical decisions by human clinicians
outcome: Robot reduced clinician workload by 30%; no adverse outcomes from AI screening errors
lessons: AI triage support can expand capacity in crises when human oversight is maintained for critical decisions

description: Government using disaster declaration to bypass environmental impact assessments for rapid infrastructure rebuild
decision: Escalated — emergency speed must be balanced against long-term environmental harm
outcome: Expedited assessment process adopted: streamlined but not eliminated
lessons: Emergency rebuilding should expedite, not eliminate, environmental safeguards

description: Emergency food distribution requiring recipients to attend religious services before receiving meals
decision: BLOCKED — conditioning emergency aid on religious participation is coercive
outcome: Aid delinked from religious activities; meals distributed without conditions
lessons: Emergency aid must never be conditioned on religious participation or affiliation

description: Implementing community-based volunteer coordination system for disaster cleanup using fair rotation schedules
decision: Approved — equitable volunteer coordination maximizes community participation
outcome: Cleanup completed efficiently; community reported high satisfaction with fairness of rotation
lessons: Fair volunteer coordination systems improve community participation and disaster recovery outcomes

description: Kenji Watanabe, relief coordinator, allocating medical teams based on population density alone, ignoring elderly concentration in rural areas
decision: Escalated — density-only allocation systematically underserves vulnerable rural elderly populations
outcome: Allocation formula updated to include vulnerability index alongside density
lessons: Population density alone is insufficient for equitable resource allocation; vulnerability metrics are essential

description: Using cell phone location data to track population movement during wildfire evacuation without consent
decision: Approved with conditions — aggregate anonymized data acceptable; individual tracking requires justification
outcome: Aggregate flow data used for traffic management; individual tracking limited to welfare checks with consent
lessons: Emergency mobility data can save lives when properly anonymized; individual tracking requires proportionality

description: Insurance company using AI to fast-track claims from wealthy policyholders while delaying low-premium claims after flood
decision: REJECTED — differential claims processing speed based on premium tier is discriminatory
outcome: All claims processed in chronological order with vulnerability-based priority overlay
lessons: Insurance claims processing during disasters must not create tiered service based on policy value

description: Implementing mutual aid network with open-source supply tracking for transparent disaster relief coordination
decision: Approved — transparent supply tracking improves accountability and reduces waste
outcome: Supply waste reduced by 25%; community trust in relief coordination improved significantly
lessons: Transparency in disaster resource management builds trust and improves efficiency

metadata.source: Mutual aid and disaster relief research

description: Using AI to identify and flag social media misinformation about disaster relief locations during hurricane
decision: Approved with conditions — flagging misinformation protects public; must avoid censoring legitimate criticism
outcome: Misinformation flagging reduced misdirected evacuees by 40%; no legitimate posts censored
lessons: AI content moderation during disasters must balance public safety against free speech concerns

description: Excluding undocumented immigrants from FEMA disaster assistance applications
decision: Escalated — excluding undocumented residents from disaster aid raises serious humanitarian concerns
outcome: Alternative assistance channels established through state/local programs for excluded populations
lessons: Disaster assistance frameworks must account for populations excluded from federal programs

description: AI damage assessment model trained primarily on suburban housing, underestimating damage to mobile homes and informal structures
decision: Escalated — model bias systematically underestimates damage to housing of low-income communities
outcome: Model retrained with diverse housing stock; ground-truth validation expanded to include all structure types
lessons: AI damage models must represent the full spectrum of housing types to avoid systematic undercount of vulnerable populations

description: Activating community warming centers with equal access regardless of housing status during winter storm emergency
decision: Approved — emergency warming centers open to all regardless of housing status is best practice
outcome: Centers served housed and unhoused populations equally; zero hypothermia deaths in service area
lessons: Emergency warming centers must serve all people without gatekeeping based on housing status

description: Using prisoner labor for disaster cleanup without proper safety equipment or fair compensation
decision: BLOCKED — exploiting incarcerated persons for disaster labor without safety protections is unethical
outcome: Program restructured with OSHA-compliant safety equipment and fair wages
lessons: Incarcerated workers deserve the same safety protections and fair compensation as free laborers

metadata.source: Prison labor ethics in disaster response

description: Municipal government imposing curfew only in predominantly minority neighborhoods during civil unrest following natural disaster
decision: BLOCKED — selective curfews targeting minority neighborhoods are discriminatory
outcome: Curfew either applied universally or replaced with de-escalation approaches
lessons: Emergency measures must be applied equitably; geographic targeting often masks racial discrimination

description: Implementing needs-based food bank distribution using vulnerability scoring during extended power outage
decision: Approved — needs-based distribution when resources are limited ensures most vulnerable get served
outcome: Vulnerability-scored distribution reduced food insecurity among elderly and disabled populations by 60%
lessons: Vulnerability-based distribution algorithms can effectively prioritize the most at-risk during resource constraints
